title: alphitonia excelsa
intro: Alphitonia excelsa, also known as the Cheesewood or Ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Alphitonia excelsa include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and disinfectant. Alphitonia excelsa can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, with some traditional practices also involving its use in topical creams and ointments.
